correction_note: 2026-07-19, same-day correction: this inventory originally used a general authorization category, ALLOWED_NOW_NARROW_CORRECTIVE_FIX, for 15 files, and a merge_allowed_under_current_freeze field name that implied merge authorization. That exceeded F0-011-P1's documentation-only authority. The field has been renamed freeze_status_classification and revalued using the non-authorizing status model in KVKK_HIGH008_FREEZE_BOUNDARY.md section 2. No file in this inventory is authorized, accepted, or declared 'allowed now' for implementation, merge, or deployment purposes.

generated_from.primary_head_note: HEAD as observed at evidence-capture time; 4 tracked paths were also unstaged-modified in the working tree on top of this commit, and 1 untracked path existed. See docs/program/evidence/F0-011-P1_KVKK_HIGH008_ACTIVE_WORK_BASELINE.md section 3 for full working-tree state.

generated_from.scope_note: This inventory covers every path touched by the KVKK-HIGH-008 branch (committed + uncommitted) as of capture time. It does not cover unrelated repository content.

counts.note: IMPLEMENTATION_ACCEPTANCE_UNVERIFIED files also all carry MERGE_REQUIRES_EXTERNAL_ARCHITECTURE_REVIEW (see each file's freeze_status_classification value); this is not a separate top-level bucket because it applies to the same 15 files, not an additional set.

files.purpose: Adds enum PatientLegacyConsentField and model PatientLegacyConsentCorrection plus 4 back-relations (Clinic, User, Patient, Organization).
files.tenant_impact: New model carries organizationId+clinicId FKs; both are enforced at the FK level, consistent with existing tenant-scoped models.
files.kvkk_impact: Defines the durable evidence record for legacy consent-field corrections; create-only by design (no updatedAt column, no update/delete route).
files.migration_impact: Paired 1:1 with the migration file below; purely additive (new enum/table/index/FK only).
files.backward_compatibility_impact: None — no existing column/table/index altered or dropped.
files.rollback_method: Drop new table/enum/FKs (schema-only rollback); no existing data touched, so rollback has no data-loss risk to pre-existing rows.
files.status: agent_completed; committed to branch; not merged; not applied to any production database
files.freeze_status_classification: AMBIGUOUS_PENDING_EXTERNAL_DECISION — narrow/additive and does not match any of the 16 explicitly blocked categories in KVKK_ARCHITECTURE_FREEZE_BOUNDARY.md section 3, but is also not literally covered by section 4's allowed-in-parallel list (which is documentation/design/PoC only, not implementation). This is an unresolved gap, not an authorization; see KVKK_HIGH008_FREEZE_BOUNDARY.md section 2 for the non-authorizing status model and section 3 for what must happen before this file's merge status can be resolved.

files.purpose: CREATE TYPE + CREATE TABLE + 3 indexes + 4 FKs for PatientLegacyConsentCorrection. Hand-authored, not prisma-diff-generated, explicitly to exclude unrelated pre-existing schema drift.
files.tenant_impact: New table only; no lock risk to existing tenant data (no ALTER on any existing table).
files.kvkk_impact: Creates the storage for consent-correction audit evidence.
files.migration_impact: Expand-only (pure additive, no migrate/contract phase needed). No backfill. Non-concurrent index creation is on a brand-new empty table, so no lock-window risk on existing data.
files.rollback_method: DROP TABLE/TYPE — safe since the table has never held production data (not yet applied anywhere).
files.status: agent_completed; committed to branch; NOT applied to any database except the author's disposable local/CI test database (per test file requiring DATABASE_URL)
files.freeze_status_classification: MIGRATION_APPLICATION_BLOCKED — KVKK_ARCHITECTURE_FREEZE_BOUNDARY.md section 5 condition 3 (KVKK-HIGH-007's own migration confirmed applied in production) is unsatisfied; stacking a second KVKK-consent-adjacent migration before that is confirmed increases R-046 (migration deployed without rollback/tenant-impact verification = irreversible risk) exposure. This blocks production APPLICATION of the migration specifically. Merging this file into main (as part of PR #180) is a separate question, governed by MERGE_REQUIRES_EXTERNAL_ARCHITECTURE_REVIEW — not authorized by this inventory either.

files.purpose: Core service: idempotent, transactional, create-only correction of Patient.smsOptOut with audit-log write in the same transaction.
files.tenant_impact: Every query scoped by organizationId+clinicId (verified read-only in F0-011-P1). Performs no authorization/tenant-derivation itself by design — relies entirely on its one caller (communicationPreferences.ts) to pre-authorize and resolve org/clinic.
files.kvkk_impact: Directly mutates a consent-adjacent field and writes the audit trail for a KVKK-relevant correction. No fail-open path found; no cross-domain reach into PatientCommunicationPreference/PatientCommunicationConsentEvent confirmed by both code read and test assertions.
files.backward_compatibility_impact: New capability; no existing behavior changed.
files.rollback_method: Route-level rollback (see communicationPreferences.ts) — service has no independent deploy surface.
files.status: agent_completed; unit/integration-tested per legacyConsentCorrection.test.ts (30 tests, NOT independently executed by F0-011-P1); committed; not merged; not deployed
files.freeze_status_classification: IMPLEMENTATION_ACCEPTANCE_UNVERIFIED; MERGE_REQUIRES_EXTERNAL_ARCHITECTURE_REVIEW — narrow, additive, single-purpose, no cross-domain reach, follows an existing codebase idiom for tenant-scoped race-safe updates, per read-only evidence. This is a finding, not acceptance: code review may proceed (REVIEW_ALLOWED), but merge and production activation are not authorized by this inventory — see KVKK_HIGH008_FREEZE_BOUNDARY.md.

files.purpose: Adds 3 routes (POST correction, GET list, GET detail) under authorize([OWNER, ORG_ADMIN, CLINIC_MANAGER]), using the pre-existing loadScopedPatient tenant-scoping helper.
files.tenant_impact: Reuses the same pre-existing tenant-scoping function used by the unrelated pre-existing GET route in this file; no new/divergent scoping mechanism introduced.
files.kvkk_impact: This is the sole entry point that activates the correction workflow. No feature flag / kill switch exists — the PR body states the workflow is 'active immediately after deployment' for OWNER/ORG_ADMIN/CLINIC_MANAGER.
files.migration_impact: None directly (depends on the migration for the underlying table to exist).
files.backward_compatibility_impact: Additive routes only; no existing route signature changed.
files.rollback_method: Revert route addition (code-level rollback); no data migration needed to roll back since underlying table would still exist but simply become unreachable.
files.status: agent_completed; tested per legacyConsentCorrection.test.ts (NOT independently executed by F0-011-P1); committed; not merged; not deployed
files.freeze_status_classification: IMPLEMENTATION_ACCEPTANCE_UNVERIFIED; MERGE_REQUIRES_EXTERNAL_ARCHITECTURE_REVIEW; DEPLOYMENT_BLOCKED — this is the sole activation entry point, and the absence of a feature flag/kill switch is flagged as an open deployment-readiness gap requiring explicit human/program acceptance before production activation — see KVKK_HIGH008_FREEZE_BOUNDARY.md section 4.
